title: 10 best herbal tinctures for jock itch
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for jock itch are concentrated liquid extracts of medicinal herbs, used to treat and prevent this common fungal infection. They offer a natural alternative to over-the-counter antifungal creams and powders. Examples of herbal tinctures that help with jock itch include tea tree, goldenseal, and calendula. Tea tree tincture has antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to soothe itching, burning, and redness. Goldenseal tincture has antimicrobial properties, aiding in preventing the spread of the infection. Calendula tincture promotes skin healing and reduces inflammation. By using herbal tinctures, individuals can find relief from symptoms and improve their quality of life without relying on synthetic treatments.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tincture helps with jock itch because it has anti-fungal and anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the skin and reduce redness and itching. The herb's active compounds, such as sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi responsible for jock itch. Yarrow tincture can be applied topically to the affected area to provide relief from symptoms and promote healing. Its astringent properties can also help to dry out the area and prevent further infection. Regular use of yarrow tincture can help to alleviate the discomfort and embarrassment associated with jock itch, allowing individuals to resume their normal activities with confidence.
remedy_properties: Anti-fungal: Yarrow tincture has anti-fungal properties that help with jock itch by inhibiting the growth of the fungus that causes the infection, thanks to the active constituents like lactones, flavonoids, and tannins.

Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tincture has anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate the itching, redness, and swelling associated with jock itch, due to the presence of flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones.
Astringent: Yarrow tincture has astringent properties that help reduce the secretion of fluids and promote healing of the affected area, as a result of its tannin content.
Analgesic: Yarrow tincture has analgesic properties that help relieve pain and discomfort caused by jock itch, due to the presence of lactones, flavonoids, and tannins.
Antiseptic: Yarrow tincture has antiseptic properties that help prevent secondary bacterial infections in the affected area, thanks to its volatile oils and tannins.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in anti-fungal and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in treating jock itch.
Leaves: Contain azulene, which has antipruritic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to soothe itching and irritation.
Roots: Packed with antifungal and antiseptic compounds, making them ideal for treating fungal infections like jock itch.
Seeds: High in essential fatty acids, which help to reduce inflammation and promote skin healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh yarrow leaves and flowers at dawn or early morning when they are most potent.
Clean and chop the harvested yarrow material into small pieces to release its medicinal properties.
Combine one part chopped yarrow with one part 95% grain alcohol in a clean glass jar to create the tincture mixture.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 14 days to allow the herbs to extract their active compounds.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and store it in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garlic tincture helps with jock itch because it has antifungal and antimicrobial properties that can effectively combat the fungus that causes this infection. The active compounds in garlic, such as allicin, can inhibit the growth and spread of the fungus, providing relief from itching, redness, and inflammation. Additionally, garlic has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the skin and reduce irritation. Its potent antifungal and antimicrobial properties make garlic tincture a natural and effective remedy for jock itch.
remedy_properties: Antifungal: Garlic tincture has strong antifungal properties that help to combat the fungal infection responsible for jock itch, thanks to its active constituents, allicin and ajoene.
Anti-inflammatory: Its anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, irritation, and swelling that often accompany jock itch, due to the presence of diallyl disulfide and s-allylcysteine.

Antibacterial: Garlic tincture inhibits bacterial growth, which can help prevent secondary bacterial infections that may occur due to scratching or poor hygiene, as a result of its antibacterial compounds, allicin and diallyl sulfide.
Analgesic: Garlic tincture's analgesic properties provide relief from itching, pain, and discomfort, which are common symptoms of jock itch, due to the presence of allicin and ajoene.

Immune-boosting: Boosting the immune system can help the body fight off fungal infections more effectively, and garlic tincture has immune-enhancing properties, attributed to its rich content of antioxidants and allicin.
remedy_parts: Bulbs: Garlic bulbs are commonly used to make medicinal tinctures for jock itch due to their potent antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes of certain garlic varieties, such as Allium sativum, are used for their antifungal and soothing effects, making them ideal for treating jock itch.
Leaves: Garlic leaves have been traditionally used in tinctures for their antifungal and antibacterial properties, providing relief from jock itch symptoms.
Flowers: Garlic flowers contain bioactive compounds that exhibit antifungal activities, making them suitable for treating jock itch when used in medicinal tinctures.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh garlic bulbs at maturity weighing about 30 grams per bulb.
Peel the garlic bulbs and chop them into small pieces to release their active compounds.
Combine one part chopped garlic with five parts 95% grain vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days allowing for maceration and extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container discarding solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kinnikinnick tincture helps with jock itch because it contains natural antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties that can alleviate the symptoms of this common skin infection. The herbs found in kinnikinnick tincture, such as bayberry, goldenseal, and myrrh, have been used for centuries to treat various skin conditions, including jock itch. These herbs work together to soothe irritation, reduce inflammation, and combat the fungus that causes jock itch. By using kinnikinnick tincture, individuals can find relief from the itching, burning, and redness associated with jock itch, all while benefiting from a natural and holistic approach to skin care.
remedy_properties: Anti-fungal: Kinnikinnick tincture contains anti-fungal properties that help to combat the fungal infection causing jock itch, thanks to its active constituents like tannins and essential oils.
Anti-inflammatory: Its anti-inflammatory properties help reduce the redness, itching, and swelling associated with jock itch, due to the presence of compounds like arbutin and flavonoids.

Astringent: Kinnikinnick tincture's astringent properties help to dry out the excess moisture in the affected area, reducing the ideal environment for fungal growth, which is primarily due to tannins.
Analgesic: The analgesic properties of kinnikinnick tincture help alleviate the pain and discomfort caused by jock itch, as it contains active constituents like terpenes and phenolic acids.

Immune-boosting: By strengthening the immune system, kinnikinnick tincture aids the body in fighting off the fungal infection, which is attributed to its rich antioxidant content and various polyphenols.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain anti-fungal and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in treating jock itch.
Leaves: Rich in tannins, which have astringent properties that can help soothe and heal irritated skin.
Bark: Has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for treating skin infections and inflammation.
Seeds: Contain essential oils and antioxidants that can help reduce redness, itching, and irritation caused by jock itch.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh arctostaphylos uva ursi leaves and stems in the early morning after the dew has dried.
Wash the harvested plant material with cold water to remove dirt and debris then pat dry gently.
Chop the cleaned plant material into small pieces then combine with vodka or grain alcohol at a ratio of 1 part plant to 2 parts solvent.
Allow the mixture to steep for 6-8 weeks in a dark glass container with a lid shaking weekly to extract active compounds.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle then store in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tincture helps with jock itch because it has antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties that can alleviate the symptoms of this skin infection. The herb's active compounds, including flavonoids and carotenoids, work together to inhibit the growth of fungi responsible for jock itch, while reducing redness, itching, and irritation. Moreover, pot marigold tincture promotes wound healing and has a soothing effect on the affected area, providing quick relief and helping to restore the skin's natural balance.
remedy_properties: Antifungal property: Pot marigold tincture inhibits the growth of fungi causing jock itch, due to the presence of active constituents like flavonoids and essential oils.
Anti-inflammatory property: It reduces inflammation and irritation in the affected area, owing to the presence of anti-inflammatory compounds like quercetin and luteolin.

Antipruritic property: Pot marigold tincture alleviates itching, an essential aspect of jock itch treatment, because of its antipruritic properties derived from active constituents like tannins and saponins.
Wound-healing property: It promotes faster healing of broken skin, reducing the risk of secondary infections, due to the presence of active constituents like triterpenoids and flavonoids.
Antibacterial property: Pot marigold tincture prevents bacterial infections in the affected area, owing to its antibacterial properties derived from active constituents like thujone and camphor.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Pot marigold flowers are used in medicinal tinctures for jock itch due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Leaves: The leaves of the pot marigold plant are used in tinctures for jock itch because of their soothing and antifungal effects.
Roots: Pot marigold roots are included in tinctures for jock itch due to their antifungal and antimicrobial properties, which help to alleviate symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula officinalis flowers when they are fully open and dry them immediately using a food dehydrator at 135°f for 3-4 hours.
Measure out one part dried calendula officinalis flowers with two parts vodka 80% abv or high-proof rum in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for four to six weeks shaking the mixture daily to facilitate extraction of bioactive compounds.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids and rinsing the filter with vodka.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store it in the refrigerator at 39°f to preserve the herbal extract for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea tincture helps with jock itch because it contains high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and heal irritated skin. The tincture is made from the leaves of the Camellia sinensis plant, which has been used in traditional medicine for centuries to treat various skin conditions. White tea's antifungal and antibacterial properties can help combat the fungus that causes jock itch, providing relief from itching, redness, and inflammation. Additionally, white tea tincture can help strengthen the skin's natural barrier, protecting it from further infection and promoting faster healing.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: White tea tincture contains active constituents, such as polyphenols and catechins, which can help inhibit the growth of fungi responsible for jock itch, like Candida albicans and Trichophyton rubrum.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tincture's anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG), can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with jock itch.

Antibacterial properties: White tea tincture's antibacterial properties, attributed to its polyphenolic compounds, can help prevent secondary bacterial infections that might occur alongside jock itch.

Antioxidant properties: The high antioxidant content, particularly from flavonoids and phenolic acids, aids in promoting skin health, healing, and rejuvenation, which is beneficial for managing jock itch symptoms.

Astringent properties: White tea tincture's astringent properties, due to its tannin content, can help constrict skin pores and tighten skin tissues, providing relief from excessive sweating and moisture accumulation in the affected area.
remedy_parts: Leaves: White tea leaves are rich in antioxidants and have antifungal properties, making them effective in treating jock itch.
Buds: The young buds of the white tea plant contain high levels of polyphenols, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the skin affected by jock itch.
Bark: The bark of the white tea plant has tannins, which have astringent properties that can help dry out the excess moisture caused by jock itch.
Roots: The roots of the white tea plant contain essential oils and antimicrobial compounds that can help eliminate the fungus causing jock itch.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality camellia sinensis leaves at peak freshness and store them in airtight containers for later use.
Combine one part dried white tea leaves with two parts pure vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 6-8 weeks in a cool dark place, shaking the jar daily to facilitate infusion and extraction.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and retaining the tincture.
Store the finished herbal white tea tincture in the refrigerator for up to 2 years, labeling and dating it for future reference.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Gotu kola tincture helps with jock itch because it has anti-fungal and anti-inflammatory properties that can effectively treat the infection causing jock itch. The main active compounds in gotu kola, asiaticoside and madecassoside, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi, including Candida albicans, the most common cause of jock itch. Additionally, gotu kola's anti-inflammatory effects can help reduce redness, itching, and irritation, providing relief from the symptoms of jock itch. The tincture form of gotu kola is also easily absorbed by the body, allowing for quick and effective treatment of jock itch.
remedy_properties: Antifungal property: Gotu kola tincture inhibits the growth of fungi responsible for jock itch, such as Trichophyton rubrum, due to the presence of triterpenoids like asiaticoside and madecassoside.
Anti-inflammatory property: Gotu kola tincture reduces inflammation and itching associated with jock itch, thanks to the compound brahmoside and other triterpenes.

Wound healing property: Gotu kola tincture promotes tissue repair and new cell growth, speeding up recovery from jock itch-related skin damage, as a result of active constituents like asiatic acid and madecassic acid.
Antipruritic property: Gotu kola tincture alleviates itching, providing relief from the discomfort caused by jock itch, due to its anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects.

Immunomodulatory property: Gotu kola tincture enhances the immune response, helping the body fight off fungal infections like jock itch, by stimulating the production of immune cells and modulating cytokine production.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of gotu kola are commonly used in making medicinal tinctures for jock itch due to their anti-fungal and anti-inflammatory properties.
Rhyzomes: Gotu kola rhyzomes are used in tinctures for jock itch because of their skin-healing and antimicrobial properties.
Flowers: The flowers of gotu kola are used in tinctures for jock itch due to their soothing and calming effects on the skin.
Seeds: Gotu kola seeds are sometimes included in tinctures for jock itch because of their potential to improve circulation and promote tissue regeneration.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh gotu kola leaves measure out 500 grams of leaf material for processing.
Dry the gotu kola leaves using shade drying or oven on low temperature method take 3-4 days.
Crush dried gotu kola leaves into smaller pieces to facilitate extraction and blending grind into fine powder.
Make the tincture by combining 1 part crushed gotu kola with 2 parts high proof grain alcohol in a glass jar steep for 6 weeks shaking daily.
Filter the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container discard solids discard any impurities.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chicory tincture helps with jock itch because it has anti-fungal and anti-inflammatory properties that can provide relief from the itching, redness, and irritation caused by the fungal infection. The active compounds in chicory, such as sesquiterpene lactones and polyphenols, can inhibit the growth of fungi and reduce inflammation, promoting the healing of the affected skin. Additionally, chicory has been shown to have immune-boosting effects, which can help the body fight off the infection and prevent its recurrence. By using chicory tincture as a natural remedy, individuals can alleviate the symptoms of jock itch and support their overall skin health.
remedy_properties: Fungicidal properties: Chicory tincture contains antifungal compounds that help to kill the fungi causing jock itch, such as Trichophyton rubrum.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Chicory tincture can reduce inflammation and soothe irritation, providing relief from the itching, burning, and redness associated with jock itch.
Antipruritic properties: Chicory tincture has compounds that help to alleviate itching, which is a key symptom of jock itch.
Antibacterial properties: Chicory tincture can help to prevent secondary bacterial infections that can occur as a result of scratching and damaging the skin.
Astringent properties: Chicory tincture can help to dry up excess moisture and reduce the dampness that can contribute to the growth and spread of the fungus responsible for jock itch.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chicory roots contain anti-fungal properties that can help alleviate symptoms of jock itch.
Flowers: Chicory flowers have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe irritation and reduce redness caused by jock itch.
Seeds: Chicory seeds are rich in antioxidants that can help boost the immune system and speed up the healing process for jock itch.
Leaves: Chicory leaves have natural antiseptic properties that can help prevent the spread of jock itch and promote skin healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried chicory roots and chop them into small pieces ensuring uniform size.
Combine one part of chopped chicory with four parts of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar filling it almost completely.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for four to six weeks allowing maceration.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding solids and pressing on solids.
Label the tincture with its name dosage and preparation instructions and store it in a cool dark place indefinitely.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tincture helps with jock itch because it has powerful anti-fungal and anti-inflammatory properties. The active ingredient in turmeric, curcumin, is known to inhibit the growth of fungi that cause jock itch. Its anti-inflammatory properties also help reduce redness, itching, and irritation caused by the infection. Moreover, turmeric tincture can be easily absorbed into the bloodstream, providing quick and effective relief. Its natural properties make it a safe and gentle alternative to over-the-counter creams and medications, making it an ideal choice for those looking for a more natural approach to treating jock itch.
remedy_properties: Antifungal property: Turmeric tincture's antifungal properties, due to curcumin and other curcuminoids, help combat the fungal infection causing jock itch.
Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory effects of curcumin in turmeric tincture alleviate itching, redness, and swelling associated with jock itch.
Antibacterial property: The antibacterial properties of turmeric tincture, attributed to curcumin and volatile oils, protect the affected area from secondary bacterial infections.
Wound healing property: Turmeric tincture's wound healing abilities, owing to curcumin and various growth factors, promote skin regeneration and help repair damaged skin caused by jock itch.
Analgesic property: The analgesic effects of curcumin in turmeric tincture reduce pain and provide relief from the discomfort caused by jock itch.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Turmeric's underground stem (rhizome) contains curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it ideal for treating jock itch.
Roots: The roots of turmeric, closely related to the rhizomes, also contain curcumin and are used interchangeably with rhizomes to make medicinal tinctures for jock itch.
Leaves: Turmeric leaves, while not as potent as the rhizomes or roots, can be used in tinctures for jock itch due to their antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: Turmeric flowers contain curcumin and other beneficial compounds, making them useful in medicinal tinctures for treating jock itch symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality turmeric roots (curcuma longa) for fresh and potent extract.
Dry the turmeric roots in a warm oven or dehydrator at low temperature settings.
Chop the dried turmeric roots into small pieces and combine with high-proof vodka or grain alcohol.
Store the mixture in a dark glass jar and allow it to steep for 6-8 weeks, shaking occasionally.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids before bottling the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horsetail tincture helps with jock itch because it contains anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help reduce redness, itching, and irritation caused by the fungal infection. The herb's high silica content can also promote skin healing and regeneration, while its astringent properties can help dry out excess moisture in the affected area. Additionally, horsetail tincture has been shown to have antifungal effects against various strains of Candida, the fungus responsible for jock itch. These combined properties make horsetail tincture a natural and effective remedy for relieving the symptoms of jock itch and promoting overall skin health.
remedy_properties: Antifungal property: Horsetail tincture contains antifungal compounds, such as phenolic acids and flavonoids, that help combat the fungal infection causing jock itch.
Astringent property: The astringent nature of horsetail tincture, due to its tannin content, helps dry out excess moisture and reduce inflammation in the affected area.

Anti-inflammatory property: Horsetail tincture has anti-inflammatory properties, attributed to its silica and saponin content, which help alleviate itching, redness, and swelling associated with jock itch.
Antimicrobial property: Horsetail tincture's antimicrobial properties, derived from its phenolic compounds, help prevent secondary bacterial infections that can occur in conjunction with jock itch.
Healing properties: The healing properties of horsetail tincture, due to its silica and antioxidant content, promote skin tissue repair and help hasten the recovery process for jock itch.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Horsetail rhizomes contain antimicrobial properties that can help to alleviate the symptoms of jock itch.
Stems: Horsetail stems have astringent and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe irritated skin and reduce redness.
Leaves: Horsetail leaves contain silica, which can help to promote skin healing and reduce the itching associated with jock itch.
Seeds: Horsetail seeds have diuretic properties that can help to eliminate toxins from the body, which can be beneficial for treating jock itch.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried equisetum arvense aerials in the spring, summer or fall seasons.
Clean and chop 200g of the harvested plant material into smaller pieces for easier extraction.
Combine the chopped plant material with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for 6 to 8 weeks for infusion.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tincture helps with jock itch because it has antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties that can provide relief from the itching, redness, and burning caused by the fungal infection. The active compounds in fennel, such as trans-anethole, fenchone, and estragole, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi responsible for jock itch. Fennel tincture can also help reduce inflammation and soothe the affected area, providing quick relief and promoting faster healing. Its antiseptic properties can also prevent secondary infections, making it an effective natural remedy for jock itch.
remedy_properties: Anti-fungal: Fennel tincture has anti-fungal properties that help with jock itch by inhibiting the growth of the fungus that causes the infection, thanks to its active constituents such as trans-anethole and fenchone.

Anti-inflammatory: Fennel tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce the redness, swelling, and itching associated with jock itch, due to the presence of compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids.
Anti-pruritic: Fennel tincture has anti-pruritic properties that alleviate itching, a common symptom of jock itch, by reducing histamine release, which is partly attributed to its anethole content.

Antibacterial: Fennel tincture's antibacterial properties help prevent secondary bacterial infections that can occur alongside jock itch, as a result of the active constituents like limonene and α-pinene.

Wound-healing: Fennel tincture promotes wound healing, aiding in the recovery from jock itch, by enhancing tissue regeneration and re-epithelialization, partly due to the presence of rosmarinic acid and other polyphenols.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are used in medicinal tinctures for jock itch due to their antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties.
Seeds: Fennel seeds are rich in antifungal compounds, making them an ideal part for creating tinctures to treat jock itch.
Leaves: The leaves of the fennel plant have antimicrobial properties, which can help soothe and heal jock itch symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and stems at dawn or early morning for maximum potency.
Wash the harvested plant material with filtered water to remove dirt and debris gently.
Chop the cleaned plant material into small pieces and combine it with 40% vodka or high-proof alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 14 days shaking it daily to allow for extraction and infusion.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids completely.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat jock itch with herbal tinctures is by using anti-fungal solutions derived from plants such as tea tree, lavender, or oregano. These natural remedies have potent properties that can help alleviate the itching, burning, and redness caused by this fungal infection. Mix a few drops of the chosen tincture with a carrier oil and apply it directly to the affected area, ensuring thorough coverage. Repeat this process two to three times a day for optimal results.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to jock itch that are treated with herbal tinctures include candidiasis, ringworm, and athlete's foot. These conditions are often caused by fungal infections, similar to jock itch. Herbal tinctures, such as those made from calendula, goldenseal, or tea tree oil, can be used to treat these ailments. These natural remedies have antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms and promote healing. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new treatment to ensure safety and effectiveness.
